steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The phrase “inadvertently overlook nuances” in the second sentence shows scholars disregard subtleties when they categorize authors into movements.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean "overlook or dismiss" — the passage says scholars may "inadvertently overlook nuances" when grouping authors into movements. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — magnify: "scholars magnify subtleties" — magnify means to enlarge or amplify; this is the opposite of overlooking. ✗
  • Choice B — scrutinize: "scholars scrutinize subtleties" — scrutinize means to examine closely and carefully, also the opposite of overlooking. ✗
  • Choice C — discount: "scholars discount subtleties" — discount means to treat as unimportant or disregard, directly matching "inadvertently overlook nuances." ✓
  • Choice D — denounce: "scholars denounce subtleties" — denounce means to publicly condemn; active condemnation is different from passively overlooking. ✗
That matches Choice C — discount.
